name: Welcome
description:
“Science, in all its senses, is a social process that both causes and is caused by social organization. To do science is to be a social actor engaged, whether one likes it or not, in political activity.”
—Richard Levins & Richard Lewontin, The Dialectical Biologist (1985)


[caption id=attachment_2393 align=aligncenter width=739] by Elizabeth Fox-Wolfe, 1972[/caption]

Science for the People (SftP) is an organization dedicated to the radical transformation of science and society. We recognize that science is neither neutral nor objective but inherently political in its entanglement with social processes. We reject the insulation of science from society and of natural science from the multitude of human knowledge. We direct practitioners of science—students, workers, educators, activists—toward struggles against exploitation, expropriation, and extermination. We insist on a science for the people.

Read the history of radical science and our organization, our principles, current activities, and join us.
